generatedSentence: A warrior, haunted by an imperfect backstory and the unjust union of ancient empires, often felt afraid of the unknown afterlife, a notion amplified by the ghostly phantasies that sometimes seemed to flicker like jaundice lanterns, making him roughly overlook his exhaustion. His parental mentor, now just a memory, had left him with an unusable visa and the difficult task to decouple from a historical legacy of sacrifice, making his overall existence feel like a wasted potential, often pondering nothings while watching his puppy. He kept a watchful eye, knowing his journey was just beginning along with others. One stormy weather night, the elemental force of a crashing shipwreck, a true nautical disaster, pulled him into a terrifying voyage, the suction of the waves trying to overtake his small barrel raft. He tried to abandon hope, but a kickass internal anthem kept him going, remembering the infinite possibilities his mentor once spoke of, an elegant thought against the inky blackness. His phone, now just a useless brick, offered no match for the powerful sea, and he had to gauge his strength, recalling how thickly the water gathered around him. He knew this impossible situation was his test. On a mysterious star-lit shore, he stumbled ashore, his jackets knitted and heavy, a hangover of sorts after the ordeal, his mind twitchy from the chaos, his memories obstructed by the struggle. He encountered a friendly legion of kangaroos, an otherworldly sight, offering apples and mints, their presence a joyful yet strangely piacular scene. He saw giant waterfalls in the distance, a forest of immense trees, and realized he had landed in a place that literatures could only begin to mythify. This was an initiator's journey, an obviously surreal theater of destiny, making him buckle up for whatever came next, beyond the realm of common sense or understanding. His new purpose began to materialize; he took up a pencil, sketching out his future, no longer afraid of the ranking he'd hold. He knew this half-formed plan had versatile counts, a sandwich of possibilities, and as he prepared to lance the unknown, he felt a haunting presence. From a store of old newspapers, he created a bookmark for his new journey, tracking his progress on a dashboard of his own design. He bid a quick 'hello' to his old self, prepared to retire his past, and with a swing of resolve, he promised himself no more drinking, no more marijuana, no more cockles of idiocy, but a single fireball of resolve, ready to face any opacity.

His parental mentor, now just a memory, had left him with an unusable visa and the difficult task to decouple from a historical legacy of sacrifice, making his overall existence feel like a wasted potential, often pondering nothings while watching his puppy. He kept a watchful eye, knowing his journey was just beginning along with others.

One stormy weather night, the elemental force of a crashing shipwreck, a true nautical disaster, pulled him into a terrifying voyage, the suction of the waves trying to overtake his small barrel raft. He tried to abandon hope, but a kickass internal anthem kept him going, remembering the infinite possibilities his mentor once spoke of, an elegant thought against the inky blackness. His phone, now just a useless brick, offered no match for the powerful sea, and he had to gauge his strength, recalling how thickly the water gathered around him. He knew this impossible situation was his test.

On a mysterious star-lit shore, he stumbled ashore, his jackets knitted and heavy, a hangover of sorts after the ordeal, his mind twitchy from the chaos, his memories obstructed by the struggle. He encountered a friendly legion of kangaroos, an otherworldly sight, offering apples and mints, their presence a joyful yet strangely piacular scene. He saw giant waterfalls in the distance, a forest of immense trees, and realized he had landed in a place that literatures could only begin to mythify. This was an initiator's journey, an obviously surreal theater of destiny, making him buckle up for whatever came next, beyond the realm of common sense or understanding.

His new purpose began to materialize; he took up a pencil, sketching out his future, no longer afraid of the ranking he'd hold. He knew this half-formed plan had versatile counts, a sandwich of possibilities, and as he prepared to lance the unknown, he felt a haunting presence. From a store of old newspapers, he created a bookmark for his new journey, tracking his progress on a dashboard of his own design. He bid a quick 'hello' to his old self, prepared to retire his past, and with a swing of resolve, he promised himself no more drinking, no more marijuana, no more cockles of idiocy, but a single fireball of resolve, ready to face any opacity.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: a lean warrior wearing a crimson, tattered cape and a silver chrome helmet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, a lone figure with a watchful gaze, standing beside a small puppy, overlooking an ancient, crumbling city at dusk, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist clinging to a makeshift barrel raft amidst colossal, crashing waves during a furious lightning storm, dramatic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, disoriented but curious, being offered apples by a friendly kangaroo in a fantastical forest with glowing waterfalls, eye-level medium shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, holding a glowing pencil, sketching on a futuristic dashboard, with a resolved expression, a subtle fireball energy emanating from his hand, close-up.

definitions.definition: A young dog, especially before sexual maturity (12-18 months)
A young rat.
A young seal.
(usually in the plural) A woman’s breast.
A (generic) thing; particularly something that is a nuisance; a sucker.

To bring forth whelps or give birth to pups.
A person fulfilling a parental role.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to a parent
Befitting a parent; affectionate; tender
Of the generation of organisms that produce a hybrid
Any small luminous dot appearing in the cloudless portion of the night sky, especially with a fixed location relative to other such dots.
A luminous celestial body, made up of plasma (particularly hydrogen and helium) and having a spherical shape. Depending on context the sun may or may not be included.
A concave polygon with regular, pointy protrusions and indentations, generally with five or six points.
An actor in a leading role.

definitions.definition: An exceptionally talented or famous person, often in a specific field; a celebrity.

definitions.definition: An asterisk (*).
A symbol used to rate hotels, films, etc. with a higher number of stars denoting better quality.

A simple dance, or part of a dance, where a group of four dancers each put their right or left hand in the middle and turn around in a circle. You call them right-hand stars or left-hand stars, depending on the hand which is in the middle.
A planet supposed to influence one's destiny.
definitions.example: What's in the stars for you today? Find out in our horoscope.

definitions.definition: A star-shaped ornament worn on the breast to indicate rank or honour.
A composition of combustible matter used in the heading of rockets, in mines, etc., which, exploding in the air, presents a starlike appearance.
To appear as a featured performer or headliner, especially in an entertainment program.
To feature (a performer or a headliner), especially in a movie or an entertainment program.
To mark with a star or asterisk.
To set or adorn with stars, or bright, radiating bodies; to bespangle.
To shine like a star.
A ship that has sunk or run aground so that it is no longer seaworthy.
An event where a ship sinks or runs aground.
Destruction; ruin; irretrievable loss
To wreck a boat through a collision or mishap.
A force which pushes matter from one space into another because the pressure inside the second space is lower than the pressure in the first.
A force holding two objects together because the pressure in the space between the items is lower than the pressure outside that space.
The process of creating an imbalance in pressure to draw matter from one place to another.
A device for removing saliva from a patient's mouth during dental operations, a saliva ejector.
Influence; "pull".
To create an imbalance in pressure between one space and another in order to draw matter between the spaces.
To draw out the contents of a space.
Feeling or causing joy.
Something trifling, or of no consequence or importance.
A trivial remark (especially in the term sweet nothings).
A nobody (insignificant person).

definitions.definition: A historical romance.
Of, concerning, or in accordance with recorded history, (particularly) as opposed to legends, myths, and fictions.

definitions.definition: Of, concerning, or in accordance with the past generally.
Of, concerning, or in accordance with the scholarly discipline of history.

definitions.definition: Forming compound adjectives with the meaning "historical/~" or "historically":

definitions.definition: The act of uniting or joining two or more things into one.

That which is united, or made one; something formed by a combination or coalition of parts or members; a confederation; a consolidated body; a league.

definitions.definition: In a rough manner; without kindness, softness, or gentleness.
Unevenly or irregularly.
Without precision or exactness; imprecise but close to in quantity or amount; approximately.

definitions.definition: To give up or relinquish control of, to surrender or to give oneself over, or to yield to one's emotions.
To desist in doing, practicing, following, holding, or adhering to; to turn away from; to permit to lapse; to renounce; to discontinue.

definitions.definition: To become greater than something else

definitions.definition: A call for response if it is not clear if anyone is present or listening, or if a telephone conversation may have been disconnected.

definitions.definition: One of two usually roughly equal parts into which anything may be divided, or considered as divided.

In some part approximating a half.
Partially; imperfectly.

definitions.definition: Observant, vigilant and aware

Having varied uses or many functions.
Changeable or inconstant.
Capable of moving freely in all directions.

definitions.definition: Capable of taking both a top and bottom role.
The act of counting or tallying a quantity.
definitions.example: Give the chairs a quick count to check if we have enough.

definitions.definition: The result of a tally that reveals the number of items in a set; a quantity counted.
A countdown.
A charge of misconduct brought in a legal proceeding.
The number of balls and strikes, respectively, on a batter's in-progress plate appearance.
definitions.example: He has a 3–2 count with the bases loaded.

definitions.definition: An object of interest or account; value; estimation.
To recite numbers in sequence.
definitions.example: Can you count to a hundred?

definitions.definition: To determine the number (of objects in a group).
definitions.example: There are three apples; count them.

definitions.definition: To be of significance; to matter.
definitions.example: Your views don’t count here.    It does count if you cheat with someone when you’re drunk.

definitions.definition: To be an example of something: often followed by as and an indefinite noun.
definitions.example: Apples count as a type of fruit.

definitions.definition: To consider something an example of something.
definitions.example: He counts himself a hero after saving the cat from the river.   I count you as more than a friend.

definitions.definition: To take account or note (of).
To plead orally; to argue a matter in court; to recite a count.
The male ruler of a county.
A nobleman holding a rank intermediate between dukes and barons.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Tanaecia. Other butterflies in this genus are called earls and viscounts.
(of a statistic) To be enumerated rather than represented as a proportion or percentage.
Antiphon.
A choral or vocal composition, often with a religious or political lyric.
definitions.example: The school's anthem sang of its many outstanding qualities, and it was hard to keep a straight face while singing.

definitions.definition: A hymn of praise or loyalty.
definitions.example: The choir sang a selection of Christmas anthems at the service just before the big day.

definitions.definition: A very popular song or track.
To celebrate with anthems.
Requiring atonement or reparation; wicked or sinful.
Expiatory.
A dish or foodstuff where two or more slices of bread serve as the wrapper or container of some other food.
(by extension) Any combination formed by layering one type of material between two layers of some other material.
A layer cake or sandwich cake.
To place one item between two other, usually flat, items
To put or set something between two others, in time.
Of a meal or serving size that is smaller than a dinner.
An impossibility
definitions.example: Late 14th century: “Madame,” quod he, “this were an impossible!” — Geoffrey Chaucer, ‘The Franklin's Tale’, Canterbury Tales

definitions.definition: Not possible; not able to be done or happen.
definitions.example: It is difficult, if not impossible, to memorize 20,000 consecutive numbers.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Very difficult to deal with.
definitions.example: You never listen to a word I say – you're impossible!

definitions.definition: Imaginary
definitions.example: impossible quantities, or imaginary numbers

definitions.definition: To collect; normally separate things.
definitions.example: I've been gathering ideas from the people I work with.

definitions.definition: To bring parts of a whole closer.
definitions.example: She gathered the shawl about her as she stepped into the cold.

definitions.definition: To infer or conclude; to know from a different source.
definitions.example: From his silence, I gathered that things had not gone well.

definitions.definition: (of a boil or sore) To be filled with pus
definitions.example: Salt water can help boils to gather and then burst.

definitions.definition: To collect molten glass on the end of a tool.
To gain; to win.
Focused, centered, achieving a state of shared mysticism.
Negative effects, such as headache or nausea, caused by previous drunkenness due to (excessive) consumption of alcohol.
definitions.example: I really enjoyed yesterday’s party, but now I have the biggest hangover – I’ll not be doing that again any time soon.

definitions.definition: Similar negative effects caused by previous excessive consumption of something else, such as a drug, coffee, sugar, etc.
An unpleasant relic left from prior events.
A sleeping arrangement, usually in homeless shelters, over a rope.
A weapon of war, consisting of a long shaft or handle and a steel blade or head; a spear carried by horsemen.
A wooden spear, sometimes hollow, used in jousting or tilting, designed to shatter on impact with the opposing knight’s armour.
A spear or harpoon used by whalers and fishermen.
A soldier armed with a lance; a lancer.
An instrument which conveys the charge of a piece of ordnance and forces it home.
(founding) A small iron rod which suspends the core of the mold in casting a shell.
One of the small paper cases filled with combustible composition, which mark the outlines of a figure.
A lancet.
To pierce with a lance, or with any similar weapon.
definitions.example: Seized the due victim, and with fury lanced Her back. Dryden.

definitions.definition: To open with a lancet; to pierce
definitions.example: to lance a vein or an abscess

definitions.definition: To throw in the manner of a lance; to lanch.
The short term state of the atmosphere at a specific time and place, including the temperature, relative humidity, cloud cover, precipitation, wind, etc.
Unpleasant or destructive atmospheric conditions, and their effects.
definitions.example: Wooden garden furniture must be well oiled as it is continuously exposed to weather.

definitions.definition: The direction from which the wind is blowing; used attributively to indicate the windward side.
A situation.
A storm; a tempest.
A light shower of rain.
To expose to the weather, or show the effects of such exposure, or to withstand such effects.
(by extension) To sustain the trying effect of; to bear up against and overcome; to endure; to resist.
To break down, of rocks and other materials, under the effects of exposure to rain, sunlight, temperature, and air.
To pass to windward in a vessel, especially to beat 'round.
definitions.example: to weather a cape; to weather another ship

definitions.definition: To endure or survive an event or action without undue damage.
definitions.example: Joshua weathered a collision with a freighter near South Africa.

definitions.definition: To place (a hawk) unhooded in the open air.
Facing towards the flow of a fluid, usually air.
A flow of water over the edge of a cliff.
A waterfall-like outpouring of liquid, smoke, etc.
definitions.example: A waterfall of mist came from the open freezer.

definitions.definition: Waterfall model
The action of drinking from a vessel without touching it with the lips, considered more sanitary for a shared vessel.
definitions.example: Hey man, can I take a waterfall from your bottle?

definitions.definition: A necktie.
A chignon.
A beard.
definitions.example: I found home a dreary place after my long absence; for half the children I had known were now wearing whiskers or waterfalls... (Mark Twain, Roughing It)

definitions.definition: To fall like a waterfall.
To drink (something) from a container by pouring it from a height so as not to touch one's lips to the rim.
In a thick manner.
Rough, aggressive; powerful and successful.
Excellent, first-class.
A dense uncultivated tract of trees and undergrowth, larger than woods.
Any dense collection or amount.
definitions.example: a forest of criticism

definitions.definition: A defined area of land set aside in England as royal hunting ground or for other privileged use; all such areas.
A graph with no cycles; i.e., a graph made up of trees.
A group of domains that are managed as a unit.
The colour forest green.
To cover an area with trees.
In a powerful manner.
An other, another (person, etc), more often rendered as another.
definitions.example: I'm afraid little Robbie does not always play well with others.

definitions.definition: The other one; the second of two.
definitions.example: One boat is not better than the other.

definitions.definition: To regard, label or treat as an "other", as not part of the same group; to view as different and alien.
To treat as different or separate; segregate; ostracise.
Other people.
definitions.example: I treat others like I treat myself.

definitions.definition: Those remaining after one or more people or items have left, or done something else, or been excluded.
definitions.example: Two decided to hide, the others surrendered.   I kept two special jars and threw away all the others.

definitions.definition: In an obvious or clearly apparent manner.
definitions.example: She was in floods of tears and obviously very upset.

definitions.definition: Used as a filler word, or to introduce information even when not obvious.
definitions.example: You weren't to know, but I've spoken to, obviously, Jim about this, and ...

definitions.definition: A vista or point that gives a beautiful view.
To offer a view (of something) from a higher position.
definitions.example: Our hotel room overlooks the lake.

definitions.definition: To fail to notice; to look over and beyond (anything) without seeing it.
definitions.example: These errors were overlooked by the proofreaders.

definitions.definition: To pretend not to have noticed (something, especially a mistake or flaw); to pass over (something) without censure or punishment.
definitions.example: I’m not willing to overlook such bad behaviour.

definitions.definition: To look down upon (something) from a place that is over or above.
definitions.example: to overlook a valley from a hill

definitions.definition: To supervise, oversee; to watch over.
definitions.example: to overlook a gang of laborers

definitions.definition: To observe or watch (someone or something) surreptitiously or secretly.
To inspect (something); to examine; to look over carefully or repeatedly.
To look upon with an evil eye; to bewitch by looking upon; to fascinate.
Characterised by or exhibiting elegance.
Characterised by minimalism and intuitiveness while preserving exactness and precision.
definitions.example: an elegant solution

definitions.definition: Fine; doing well.
Of the colour of ink, especially black ink; dark.
Spattered or stained with ink.
Dark-skinned; black.
A building or institution where money (originally, only coins) is produced under government licence.
A large amount of money. A vast sum or amount, etc.
definitions.example: It must have cost a mint to produce!

definitions.definition: Any place regarded as a source of unlimited supply; the supply itself.
To reproduce (coins), usually en masse, under licence.
To invent; to forge; to fabricate; to fashion.
Any plant in the genus Mentha in the family Lamiaceae, typically aromatic with square stems.
The flavouring of the plant, either a sweet, a jelly or sauce.
Any plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae.
A green colour, like that of mint.
A mint-flavored candy, often eaten to sweeten the smell of the breath.
(provincial) Intent, purpose; an attempt, try; effort, endeavor.
(provincial) To try, attempt; take aim.
(provincial) To try, attempt, endeavor; to take aim at; to try to hit; to purpose.
To hint; suggest; insinuate.
A measure; a standard of measure; an instrument to determine dimensions, distance, or capacity; a standard
An act of measuring.
An estimate.
Any instrument for ascertaining or regulating the level, state, dimensions or forms of things
A thickness of sheet metal or wire designated by any of several numbering schemes.
The distance between the rails of a railway.
A semi-norm; a function that assigns a non-negative size to all vectors in a vector space.
The number of stitches per inch, centimetre, or other unit of distance.
Relative positions of two or more vessels with reference to the wind.
definitions.example: A vessel has the weather gauge of another when on the windward side of it, and the lee gauge when on the lee side of it.

definitions.definition: The depth to which a vessel sinks in the water.
(plastering) The quantity of plaster of Paris used with common plaster to make it set more quickly.
That part of a shingle, slate, or tile, which is exposed to the weather, when laid; also, one course of such shingles, slates, or tiles.
A unit of measurement which describes how many spheres of bore diameter of a shotgun can be had from one pound of lead; 12 gauge is roughly equivalent to .75 caliber.
(by extension) A shotgun (synecdoche for 12 gauge shotgun, the most common chambering for combat and hunting shotguns).
A tunnel-like ear piercing consisting of a hollow ring embedded in the lobe.
To measure or determine with a gauge; to measure the capacity of.
To estimate.
To appraise the character or ability of; to judge of.
To draw into equidistant gathers by running a thread through it.
To mix (a quantity of ordinary plaster) with a quantity of plaster of Paris.
To chip, hew or polish (stones, bricks, etc) to a standard size and/or shape.
Relating to or involving ships or shipping or navigation or seamen.
definitions.example: nautical charts

definitions.definition: The body of all written works.
The collected creative writing of a nation, people, group or culture.
(usually preceded by the) All the papers, treatises etc. published in academic journals on a particular subject.
Written fiction of a high standard.
definitions.example: However, even “literary” science fiction rarely qualifies as literature, because it treats characters as sets of traits rather than as fully realized human beings with unique life stories. —Adam Cadre, 2008

definitions.definition: A round vessel or cask, of greater length than breadth, and bulging in the middle, made of staves bound with hoops, and having flat ends or heads. Sometimes applied to a similar cylindrical container made of metal, usually called a drum.
definitions.example: a cracker barrel

definitions.definition: The quantity which constitutes a full barrel: the volume or weight this represents varies by local law and custom.
A solid drum, or a hollow cylinder or case
definitions.example: the barrel of a windlass;  the barrel of a watch, within which the spring is coiled.

definitions.definition: A metallic tube, as of a gun, from which a projectile is discharged.
A tube.
The hollow basal part of a feather.
The part of a clarinet which connects the mouthpiece and upper joint, and looks rather like a barrel (1).
A wave that breaks with a hollow compartment.
A waste receptacle.
definitions.example: Throw it into the trash barrel.

definitions.definition: The ribs and belly of a horse or pony.
A jar.
Any of the dark-staining regions in the somatosensory cortex of rodents, etc., where somatosensory inputs from the contralateral side of the body come in from the thalamus.
A statistic derived from launch angle and exit velocity of a ball hit in play.
To put or to pack in a barrel or barrels.
To move quickly or in an uncontrolled manner.
definitions.example: He came barrelling around the corner and I almost hit him.

definitions.definition: A strip of material used to mark a place in a book.
A record of the address of a file or Internet page serving as a shortcut to it.
A pointer found in a nonclustered index to a row in a clustered index or a table heap
To create a bookmark.
The act of retiring, or the state of being retired.
A place to which one retires.
A call sounded on a bugle, announcing to skirmishers that they are to retire, or fall back.
definitions.example: At the retire, the cavalry fell back.

definitions.definition: To stop working on a permanent basis, usually because of old age or illness.
definitions.example: Having made a large fortune, he retired.

definitions.definition: (sometimes reflexive) To withdraw; to take away.
To cease use or production of something.
definitions.example: The steamship made thousands of trips over several decades before it was retired by the shipping company.

definitions.definition: To withdraw from circulation, or from the market; to take up and pay.
definitions.example: The central bank retired those notes five years ago.

definitions.definition: To cause to retire; specifically, to designate as no longer qualified for active service; to place on the retired list.
definitions.example: The board retired the old major.

definitions.definition: (of a batsman) To voluntarily stop batting before being dismissed so that the next batsman can bat.
definitions.example: Jones retired in favour of Smith.

definitions.definition: (of a fielder) To make a play which results in a runner or the batter being out, either by means of a put out, fly out or strikeout.
definitions.example: Jones retired Smith 6-3.

definitions.definition: To go back or return; to withdraw or retreat, especially from public view; to go into privacy.
definitions.example: I will retire to the study.

definitions.definition: To retreat from action or danger; to withdraw for safety or pleasure.
definitions.example: The regiment retired from the fray after the Major was killed.

definitions.definition: To recede; to fall or bend back.
definitions.example: Past the point, the shore retires into a sequence of coves.

definitions.definition: To go to bed.
definitions.example: I will retire for the night.

definitions.definition: To remove or cease to use.
definitions.example: When a hurricane becomes so deadly or destructive that future use would be insensitive, officials may retire the name of the hurricane.

definitions.definition: To consume (a liquid) through the mouth.
definitions.example: He drank the water I gave him.

definitions.definition: (metonymic) To consume the liquid contained within (a bottle, glass, etc.).
definitions.example: Jack drank the whole bottle by himself.

definitions.definition: To consume alcoholic beverages.
definitions.example: Everyone who is drinking is drinking, but not everyone who is drinking is drinking.

definitions.definition: To take in (a liquid), in any manner; to suck up; to absorb; to imbibe.
To take in; to receive within one, through the senses; to inhale; to hear; to see.
To smoke, as tobacco.
An act or session by which drink is consumed, especially alcoholic beverages.
A panel under the windscreen of a motor car or aircraft, containing indicator dials, compartments, and sometimes controls.

An upturned screen of wood or leather placed on the front of a horse-drawn carriage, sleigh or other vehicle that protected the driver from mud, debris, water and snow thrown up by the horse's hooves.
A graphical user interface in the form of or resembling a motor car dashboard.
definitions.example: Log in to the dashboard to change your account settings.

definitions.definition: The float of a paddle wheel.
To organize in a dashboard format.
definitions.example: Dashboarding your work can enhance productivity.

definitions.definition: A member of the Macropodidae family of large marsupials with strong hind legs for hopping, native to Australia.
A hooded jacket with a front pocket, usually of fleece material, a kangaroo jacket.
To practice kangaroo care on an infant; to hold a premature infant against the skin.
To hunt kangaroo.
To move like a kangaroo
A game which is of no consequence in terms of ranking, betting etc.
definitions.example: This match is merely a friendly, so don't worry too much about it.

definitions.definition: A person or entity on the same side in a conflict.
Generally warm, approachable and easy to relate with in character.
definitions.example: Your cat seems very friendly.

definitions.definition: Inviting, characteristic of friendliness.
definitions.example: He gave a friendly smile.

definitions.definition: Having an easy or accepting relationship with something.
definitions.example: a dog-friendly café

definitions.definition: Without any hostility.
definitions.example: a friendly competition

definitions.definition: Promoting the good of any person; favourable; propitious.
definitions.example: a friendly breeze or gale

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to friendlies (friendly noun sense 2, below). Also applied to other bipolar confrontations, such as team sports
definitions.example: The soldier was killed by friendly fire.

definitions.definition: Being or relating to two or more natural numbers with a common abundancy.
definitions.example: friendly

definitions.definition: (in compounds) Compatible with, or not damaging to (the compounded noun).
definitions.example: Organic farms only use soil-friendly fertilisers.

definitions.definition: A ball of fire, especially one associated with an explosion.
A meteor bright enough to cast shadows.
A class of sailing dinghy with a single trapeze and a symmetrical spinnaker, sailed by a crew of two.
A charge made of a disc-shaped bomb shell, with four sets of flames, at the top, bottom and on either side.
A ball filled with combustibles to be thrown among enemies.
To explode in a ball of flame.
definitions.example: The car swerved off a road, hit a wall, and fireballed as the petrol tank exploded.

definitions.definition: To pitch the ball very fast.
To attack with balls of fire.
A common, round fruit produced by the tree Malus domestica, cultivated in temperate climates.

Any of various tree-borne fruits or vegetables especially considered as resembling an apple; also (with qualifying words) used to form the names of other specific fruits such as custard apple, rose apple, thorn apple etc.
The fruit of the Tree of Knowledge, eaten by Adam and Eve according to post-Biblical Christian tradition; the forbidden fruit.
A tree of the genus Malus, especially one cultivated for its edible fruit; the apple tree.
The wood of the apple tree.
(in the plural) Short for apples and pears, slang for stairs.
The ball in baseball.
When smiling, the round, fleshy part of the cheeks between the eyes and the corners of the mouth.
A Native American or red-skinned person who acts and/or thinks like a white (Caucasian) person.
(ice hockey slang) An assist.
To become apple-like.
To form buds.
The state or quality of being opaque, not allowing light to pass through
definitions.example: We could not see the sandbar due to the opacity of the muddy water.

definitions.definition: The state or quality of being inaccessible to understanding.
definitions.example: The opacity of these decisions is troubling.

definitions.definition: A measure of relative impenetrability to electromagnetic radiation such as light.
definitions.example: The opacity of a clean glass window is near zero.

definitions.definition: Of, concerned with, or preoccupied with a different world than that of the tangible here and now, such as a heavenly, spiritual, or imaginary world.
Not belonging to the real world; unnatural; odd and unfamiliar.
One who initiates.
A substance that initiates a chain reaction or polymerization.
A task (in a mainframe computer) that initiates multiple jobs.
A material whose presence in the body eventually leads to cancer.
Cotton or rayon cloth with shaded effect
A publication, usually published daily or weekly and usually printed on cheap, low-quality paper, containing news and other articles.
A quantity of or one of the types of paper on which newspapers are printed.
To cover with newspaper.
definitions.example: She newspapered one end of the room before painting the bookcase.

definitions.definition: To engage in the business of journalism (usually used only in the gerund, newspapering)
definitions.example: He newspapered his way through the South on the sports beat, avoiding dry towns.

definitions.definition: To harass in newspaper articles.
definitions.example: He was newspapered out of public life.

definitions.definition: To inhabit, or visit frequently (most often used in reference to ghosts).
definitions.example: A couple of ghosts haunt the old, burnt-down house.

definitions.definition: To make uneasy, restless.
definitions.example: The memory of his past failures haunted him.

definitions.definition: To stalk, to follow
definitions.example: The policeman haunted him, following him everywhere.

definitions.definition: To live habitually; to stay, to remain.
To accustom; habituate; make accustomed to.
To practise; to devote oneself to.
To persist in staying or visiting.
A particular instance of haunting; a ghostly habitation.
definitions.example: During the haunting, strange voices and noises were heard and objects flew off tables.

definitions.definition: Remaining in the mind; not easily forgotten.
definitions.example: He had a haunting gaze.

definitions.definition: To distort or collapse under physical pressure; especially, of a slender structure in compression.
To make bend; to cause to become distorted.
To give in; to react suddenly or adversely to stress or pressure (of a person).
definitions.example: It is amazing that he has never buckled after so many years of doing such urgent work.

definitions.definition: To yield; to give way; to cease opposing.
To enter upon some labour or contest; to join in close fight; to contend.
To buckle down; to apply oneself.
To continue over time.
To follow the tracks of.
definitions.example: My uncle spent all day tracking the deer, whose hoofprints were clear in the mud.

definitions.definition: To make tracks on.
To create a musical recording (a track).
definitions.example: Lil Kyle is gonna track with that DJ next week.

definitions.definition: To make sense; to be consistent with known information
The act or process by which something is tracked.
A consistent adjustment of space between individual letters; letterspacing.
The division of pupils into separately taught groups by perceived ability level.
A long journey, especially by ship.
The act or practice of travelling.
To go on a long journey.
An unexpected natural or man-made catastrophe of substantial extent causing significant physical damage or destruction, loss of life or sometimes permanent change to the natural environment.
definitions.example: People would suffer disasters when society's morality degenerates.

definitions.definition: An unforeseen event causing great loss, upset or unpleasantness of whatever kind.
To turn thread or yarn into a piece of fabric by forming loops that are pulled through each other. This can be done by hand with needles or by machine.
definitions.example: The first generation knitted to order; the second still knits for its own use; the next leaves knitting to industrial manufacturers.

definitions.definition: To join closely and firmly together.
definitions.example: The fight for survival knitted the men closely together.

definitions.definition: To become closely and firmly joined; become compacted.
To grow together.
definitions.example: All those seedlings knitted into a kaleidoscopic border.

definitions.definition: To combine from various elements.
definitions.example: The witness knitted together his testimony from contradictory pieces of hearsay.

definitions.definition: Of bones: to heal following a fracture.
definitions.example: I’ll go skiing again after my bones knit.

definitions.definition: To form into a knot, or into knots; to tie together, as cord; to fasten by tying.
To draw together; to contract into wrinkles.
Made by knitting, or resembling in texture something made by knitting.
The descriptive heading or title of a document or part therof
A title or brief explanation attached to an illustration, cartoon, user interface element, etc.
A piece of text appearing on screen as subtitle or other part of a film or broadcast.
The section on an official paper that describes when, where, what was taken, found or executed, and by whom it was authorized.
A seizure or capture, especially of tangible property (chattel).
To collide with something destructively, fall or come down violently.
To severely damage or destroy something by causing it to collide with something else.
definitions.example: I'm sorry for crashing the bike into a wall. I'll pay for repairs.

definitions.definition: (via gatecrash) To attend a social event without invitation, usually with unfavorable intentions.
definitions.example: We weren't invited to the party so we decided to crash it.

definitions.definition: To accelerate a project or a task or its schedule by devoting more resources to it.
To make or experience informal temporary living arrangements, especially overnight.
definitions.example: Hey dude, can I crash at your pad?

definitions.definition: To give, as a favor.
To lie down for a long rest, sleep or nap, as from tiredness or exhaustion.
(hardware) To terminate extraordinarily.
definitions.example: If the system crashes again, we'll have it fixed in the computer shop.

definitions.definition: (hardware) To cause to terminate extraordinarily.
definitions.example: Double-clicking this icon crashes the desktop.

definitions.definition: To experience a period of depression and/or lethargy after a period of euphoria, as after the euphoric effect of a psychotropic drug has dissipated.
To hit or strike with force
(of a patient's condition) To take a sudden and severe turn for the worse; to rapidly deteriorate.
To make a sudden loud noise.
definitions.example: Thunder crashed directly overhead.

definitions.definition: The sound or action of something that crashes.
definitions.example: the ceaseless crashings of waves on the beach

definitions.definition: Any of various edible European bivalve mollusks, of the family Cardiidae, having heart-shaped shells.
The shell of such a mollusk.
(in the plural) One’s innermost feelings (only in the expression “the cockles of one’s heart”).
(directly from French coquille) A wrinkle, pucker
(by extension) A defect in sheepskin; firm dark nodules caused by the bites of keds on live sheep
The mineral black tourmaline or schorl.
The fire chamber of a furnace.
A kiln for drying hops; an oast.
The dome of a heating furnace.
To cause to contract into wrinkles or ridges, as some kinds of cloth after a wetting; to pucker.
Any of several field weeds, such as the common corncockle (Agrostemma githago) and darnel ryegrass (Lolium temulentum).
A £10 note; a tenner.
A device for transmitting conversations and other sounds in real time across distances, now often a small portable unit also capable of running software etc.
To call (someone) using a telephone.
To block or fill (a passage) with obstacles or an obstacle.
To impede, retard, or interfere with; hinder.
definitions.example: They obstructed my progress.

definitions.definition: To get in the way of so as to hide from sight.
The manner in which something is swung.
definitions.example: Door swing indicates direction the door opens.

definitions.definition: The sweep or compass of a swinging body.
A line, cord, or other thing suspended and hanging loose, upon which anything may swing.
A hanging seat in a children's playground, for acrobats in a circus, or on a porch for relaxing.
A dance style.
The genre of music associated with this dance style.
The amount of change towards or away from something.
Sideways movement of the ball as it flies through the air.
Capacity of a turning lathe, as determined by the diameter of the largest object that can be turned in it.
In a musical theater production, a performer who understudies several roles.
A basic dance step in which a pair link hands and turn round together in a circle.
Free course; unrestrained liberty.
Influence or power of anything put in motion.
A type of hook with the arm more extended.
To rotate about an off-centre fixed point.
definitions.example: The plant swung in the breeze.

definitions.definition: To dance.
To ride on a swing.
definitions.example: The children laughed as they swung.

definitions.definition: To participate in the swinging lifestyle; to participate in wife-swapping.
To hang from the gallows.
(of a ball) to move sideways in its trajectory.
To fluctuate or change.
definitions.example: It wasn't long before the crowd's mood swung towards restless irritability.

definitions.definition: To move (an object) backward and forward; to wave.
definitions.example: He swung his sword as hard as he could.

definitions.definition: To change (a numerical result); especially to change the outcome of an election.
To make (something) work; especially to afford (something) financially.
definitions.example: If it’s not too expensive, I think we can swing it.

definitions.definition: To play notes that are in pairs by making the first of the pair slightly longer than written (augmentation) and the second shorter, resulting in a bouncy, uneven rhythm.
(of a bowler) to make the ball move sideways in its trajectory.
To move one's arm in a punching motion.
In dancing, to turn around in a small circle with one's partner, holding hands or arms.
definitions.example: "to swing one's partner", or simply "to swing"

definitions.definition: To admit or turn something for the purpose of shaping it; said of a lathe.
definitions.example: The lathe can swing a pulley of 12 inches diameter.

definitions.definition: To put (a door, gate, etc.) on hinges so that it can swing or turn.
To turn round by action of wind or tide when at anchor.
definitions.example: A ship swings with the tide.

definitions.definition: The state or condition of being an idiot; the quality of having an intelligence level far below average; mental retardation.
An act lacking intelligence or sense; an instance of senselessness; extremely foolish behaviour.
definitions.example: The administrators, growing tired of such idiocy, put a new policy in place.

definitions.definition: To make mythical.
